programme.desc: A murder at a Turkish baths seems like an open-and-shut case, as witnesses put an ex-serviceman at the scene of the crime and the suspect has left his ID in a locker. But as Gently and Bacchus begin their search for the man, things soon become complicated. His psychiatrist reveals he was showing signs of schizophrenia, while his brother claims he was far too soft to have killed a man - and when a second body turns up, the duo are led into a world of medical secrets at an Army testing facility. Crime drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with William Ash and Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Following his recovery from a life-threatening head injury, Leo's first case involves the shattered body of a woman found at the bottom of a block of derelict flats. A post-mortem indicates suicide, but the investigation is reopened when the pathologist uncovers evidence that the victim was shot before being thrown off the roof, and his suspicions are aroused when DI Neill seems reluctant to pursue the possibility that the deceased was an informant for an undercover police officer.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report from Salford as the city lifts the lockdown and tries to get its community back to work. The programme follows the mayor and his team, looking at the burden that has fallen on local councils, and also showing the work done by teachers, bus drivers and those helping the homeless. But for Salford it has been expensive. With the threat of bankruptcy looming, can the council provide the support and services the locals need to get them through the next phase of the health and economic crisis?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Last night saw actress Felicity Montagu, singer Lady Leshurr and presenters Amar Latif, Gethin Jones and Jeff Brazier enter the fray. But one of them has already been voted out, leaving the other four to cook on. They begin with the pairs task, for which they are split into teams and have to serve up identical dishes - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at the Wellcome Genome Campus in Cambridgeshire.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control.(n)

programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Fed up with Cathy festering in her bed, post-wedding cancellation, and stalking Marcus and Meredith on what was supposed to be Cathy and Marcus' honeymoon, Cat decides the best thing to cheer up her half-sibling is to take her away on their own 'sister honeymoon'. She's got just the place in mind - their late Dad's favourite pub. At the solicitors, Marilyn and Tess learn that Colin's will contains an absolute bombshell and that they must pass on some shocking news to their daughters. Comedy, starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Siobhan Finneran and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades narrates an insight into the maritime history of three items restored on programme - a precious box, a book of cuttings about Captain Scott, and a handmade teddy bear.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Thailand, from the northern city of Chiang Mai to Kanchanaburi on the River Kwai and from Bangkok to the southern beach resort of Hua Hin. Along the way, he tries his hand at the art of umbrella-making and learns about northern Thai cookery. He also stops off to help at a hospital for elephants wounded by landmines and admires the ruined temples at the former Siamese capital, Ayutthaya.(n)

programme.title: Jungle Atlantis
programme.desc: A thousand years ago, the Khmer people of Cambodia built an empire and their capital was the great city of Angkor, with its centrepiece being Angkor Wat - a vast temple complex covering an area more than four times the size of the Vatican City. In the first of this two-part programme, an international team of archaeologists and scientists use revolutionary technology to reveal the true scale and extent of the lost metropolis and find out how its people lived and died.(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond.(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The fifth leg of Si King and Dave Myers' tour takes them to Austria, the home of Mozart, The Sound of Music and Arnold Schwarzenegger. They start off in Vienna, the patisserie capital of the world, where they are spoilt for choice by the number of different cakes on offer. Moving on, they make authentic apfelstrudel with the owner of a roadside B&B, bake Linzertorte, cook breakfast in a monastery and make a chocolate gugelhupf cake for a group of fellow bikers from Huddersfield. Being in the Alps, they also take time out from the cooking to learn how to yodel.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring China's wildlife continues with a journey to the Yunnan Province, where parallel mountain ranges channel mighty rivers from Tibet southward to the tropics, and the landscape provides a home to diverse species. While the mountain forests are home to red pandas, the valleys below contain jungles inhabited by gibbons, sunbirds and elephants.(n)

programme.title: World Beaters
programme.desc: A profile of gymnastics sensation Simone Biles, who won four golds at the 2016 Olympics in Rio de Janeiro.(n)

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Sue Barker narrates the second part of a look back at the Games, featuring days seven to 10, with Usain Bolt, Michael Phelps and Chris Hoy among those involved.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Burnley v Wolverhampton Wanderers (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the first half of the Premier League fixture at Turf Moor, as the Clarets host Nuno Espirito Santo's Wolves. The sides shared the spoils in the reverse fixture, which took place back in August. Ashley Barnes gave Sean Dyche's men an early lead, but the home team rescued a point thanks to Raul Jiminez's penalty deep into stoppage time.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Ellis family enjoys the new freedoms of the 1960s, from steak and chips and caravan holidays to muscle man competitions. Cameras follow dad as he puts in a shift down the local pit, while mum and girls head to the local phone box to try the new telephone recipe service. Presented by Sara Cox and Polly Russell.(n)

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part one of two. Conservationist Giles Clark embarks on his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. Giles has been asked by good friend Matt Hunt, CEO of Free the Bears, to help for 12 months and is not long into his role when he has to step in to help Mary, a five-month-old sun bear. Mary was rescued after her mother was killed in the wild and was spotted on the back of a truck for sale into the illegal wildlife trade. Fragile and malnourished, she lives with Giles and Matt at home while she needs round-the-clock specialist care.(n)

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: As Phyllis's campaign to stop the ratification of the Equal Rights Amendment gathers support and momentum, its proponents prepare for the Democratic presidential campaign of 1972 and have a stark choice to make - the popular crowd-pleaser George McGovern or the trail blazing African-American Shirley Chisholm, who is determined to fight against a traditional white male preserve. Drama about the political movement to pass the Equal Rights Amendment in the US. Starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks.(n)

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: In January 1973, the Supreme Court makes an historic judgement legalising abortion in the United States. The Womens' Caucus is in a buoyant mood and agrees not to confront Phyllis Schlafly's Stop the ERA campaign head-on, fearing that it would simply feed their publicity machine. Betty Friedan has other ideas and accepts an invitation to debate Schlafly at the University of Illinois. Drama about the political movement to pass the Equal Rights Amendment in the US. Starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels the to the icy summit of Britain's highest and most wintery mountain, Ben Nevis, to discover how Scotland was first promoted as a winter holiday destination.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Emily Maitlis presents analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: Documentary examining the realities of war and life under Isis, with unique personal archive from civilians and soldiers from both sides of the conflict. As war began, many Iraqis were hopeful. Ahmed Al Bashir, now a renowned comedian, remembers practising his English with US troops before the realities of occupation became clear. Waleed Neysif was 18 when George Bush gave Saddam Hussein just 48 hours to leave Iraq. He was, like many Iraqi teenagers at that time, infatuated by the West. But while many of his generation grew up enjoying songs by the Backstreet Boys, Waleed formed Iraq's first heavy metal band. Narrated by Andy Serkis.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including the husband-and-wife slimmer of the year champions, who are looking for outfits suitable for a glitzy, gold-themed ball, and 24-year-old Bournemouth woman Grace, who would like a wardrobe suitable for her new high-flying job. The stylists also get to choose over Jill, a 56-year-old raver who is looking for a style she can throw some serious shapes in, and Mel from Doncaster, who is keen to swap her all-weather obsession with flip flops and leggings for a sophisticated new look.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monaco's only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanie's daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros' worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The battle for the first two semi-final places in this year's competition reaches its climax, as the four remaining celebrities are tasked with cooking a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, but also by three previous contenders - Olympic long-jumper Greg Rutherford, and TV personalities Vicky Pattison and Dom Parker. All three know what it takes to get through this competition and are in for a treat as their successors pull out all the stops to try to secure their semi-final place. For those who don't cut the mustard, the journey ends here.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades uses archive and three barn fixes - a centuries-old clock, a toy and clocking-off machine - to explore how the invention of clockwork continues to influence modern life.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with the Heinz baked bean factory in Wigan, which has upped production to deliver almost 50 million cans in just a month, nearly double the number of tins the firm would normally sell in the same period. Operations manager John Brockley reveals how staff are keeping safe across the site, including passing through a thermal camera that automatically takes the temperature of workers as they enter the factory.(n)

programme.title: Jungle Atlantis
programme.desc: Part two of two. The experts continue their attempt to discover why Angkor suddenly collapsed, using a revolutionary technology called lidar to reveal the true scale and splendour of this abandoned megacity. Deep in the Cambodian jungle, they have discovered a sophisticated network of roads and canals that are the forgotten world of Angkor's greatest king, shedding new light on the dramatic events leading to the fall of the city.(n)

programme.title: Coast

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys

He explores the origins of Sydney as a penal colony, visits the Hyde Park barracks where many of the first convicts were housed and volunteers for the lifesaving club at Bondi Beach.(n)

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day one of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. This is the first meeting between the sides here in this format since June 2007, when England prevailed by 60 runs. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley presents the return of the contest in which aspiring make-up artists battle it out. Their first assignment is an advertising campaign for sports fashion retailer JD. Guest judge Min Sandhu reminds the amateurs that the make-up needs to be perfect but cannot distract from the clothing. The creative brief sees the artists producing looks based on their own identity - leading to transformations ranging from a prosthetics-based alien look to a mythical phoenix. Only then do the judges decide which two will take on the first elimination face-off.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of reflections this week, with Joe Lycett, Zoe Lyons, Liza Tarbuck and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones.(n)

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Rosie is obsessed with Christmas, despite the fact that it is only mid-February, forever wanting to watch festive films on repeat and even dragging Simon to buy a tree. So when she finds another special day marked on the calendar - President's Day at the end of February - her parents wonder if celebrating that instead will help distract Rosie from her yuletide obsession. In the flashbacks to 2008, Emily and Simon are no longer talking, except to argue. But eventually, months of pent-up frustration comes to a head in an emotional showdown.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: When Nandor and Laszlo are abducted by a coven of witches, the others must venture to Brooklyn to rescue them.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Three-part documentary about the media mogul's influence on international politics and the power struggles within his own family. The first episode focuses on the 1997 general election, where Rupert Murdoch had to decide if his business interests were best served by the continuation of John Major's struggling government, or a new Labour leadership under Tony Blair. Eventually, the full force of his media empire is placed behind the rising Labour leader, but some question how high a price was paid. Elsewhere, Murdoch tries to decide which of his children will be the heir to his empire, and ambitious editor Rebekah Wade begins her rise to influence within the organisation.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of the show's most passionate contestants, including a health food entrepreneur who confused the panel with the precise composition of his low carb noodles, a couple who withhold a vital piece of information while pitching their iced tea company, and a former boxer-turned-inventor.(n)

programme.title: Italy's Frontline: A Doctor's Diary
programme.desc: Documentary featuring an unprecedented and intimate insight into three months of life on the Covid frontline in Italy with A&E doctor Francesca Mangiatordi. In the early days of the outbreak, the hospital is overflowing and Francesa works gruelling 12-hour shifts caring for those who have been most affected. Due to a lack of life-saving equipment she faces the traumatising task of prioritising which lives to try to save. At home, Francesca is fearful of infecting her husband, who suffers from a respiratory condition, and she hasn't touched him - or her children - for more than a month. Narrated by Florence Pugh.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer and a textile artist pitch ideas for a Derby couple's wedding, with only two being made before the buyers choose their favourite.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The importance of music in people's lives, through the restoration of a pump organ, an accordion and a bass guitar owned by a member of 1970s disco group Hot Chocolate.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes west across Manitoba into the province of Saskatchewan. High above the Prairie at Riding Mountain, he discovers how a middle-class British boy from Hastings transformed himself into an influential indigenous naturalist called Grey Owl. Deep in the Prairie, he finds a network of railways that once served the wheat farmers of Saskatchewan, and learns how communities grew up around the grain elevators used to load the crops on to rail wagons.(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions.(n)

programme.title: The Open: Chronicles of a Champion Golfer
programme.desc: A look at how Darren Clarke, Nick Faldo and Rory McIlroy have fared at The Open, featuring contributions from all three golfers.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Hermione Cockburn discovers the secrets to the success of British seaside towns on a visit to Blackpool's Pleasure Beach, and looks back at Rhyl's glory years through Victorian postcards. Neil Oliver also finds out about the people who manned Liverpool's dockyards.(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob looks back at the 2012 Olympics opening ceremony, with Danny Boyle and the creative team explaining the challenges that went into creating 'The Greatest Show on Earth'. With exclusive access to behind-the-scenes footage, from auditions through to the night itself, this is the story of 90 minutes that dazzled the world, including interviews with some of the 7,500 volunteers involved in the show, who discuss the friendships that were forged during the show and how it has affected their lives since.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Olympic Torch Story
programme.desc: A look back at the 70-day, 8,000-mile nationwide relay that captured the public's imagination in the build-up to the 2012 London Olympics, hearing from some of the 8,000 torchbearers. The film recalls moments including the torch zip-wiring across the Tyne Bridge, crossing the border in Ireland, climbing Mount Snowdon, being part of a marriage proposal and joining in ballroom dancing in Blackpool.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher are in Ushaw in County Durham, seeking a selection of objects to take to auction. Catherine falls in love with a model of Muffin the Mule and David is enchanted by a limited edition book by author JK Rowling. Presenter Paul Martin learns what life was like for the students who studied at Ushaw when it was open as a Catholic college.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Portillo continues his 570-mile journey through New South Wales. In the Blue Mountains, he learns about people's relationship to the land from his indigenous guide, before reaching Katoomba, where he rides the steepest railway in the world. He follows this with an overnight journey on one of the world's longest trains, the luxurious Indian Pacific Railway, which takes him to his destination - the mining city of Broken Hill.(n)

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day two of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. It is the first time this venue has hosted a Test match since last September, when England lost the Fourth Test of the 2019 Ashes series to Australia. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost brings viewers up-to-date on how his garden has been developing over the summer as well as offering advice on looking after and growing hydrangeas. In Devon, Toby Buckland shares tips on dealing with damaged shrubs, tree pruning and transplanting perennials, while Frances Tophill travels to West Sussex to meet a gardener and professor of biology whose garden has been planted with the conservation of insects in mind. Plus, a second chance to see Carol Klein's in-depth profile of a national collection of hydrangeas at Darley Park in Derby.(n)

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region.(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Singer Adam Ant presents a retrospective look at the pop culture of 1981, when Kim Wilde, Clare Grogan and Tom Selleck were among the pin-ups. It was the year Britain was introduced to animated crimefighter Danger Mouse, the sport of darts was in its heyday, and the films Gregory's Girl and Porky's hit cinemas.(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: After reflecting on the fallout of the first week, Louise discovers something troubling about Tom. Rosamund Pike and Chris O'Dowd star in a comedy, written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban presents analysis of the day's national and international events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: 20th Century Women (2016)
programme.desc: Comedy drama following a teenage boy, his mother and two other women who help raise him among the love and freedom of Southern California in 1979. Starring Annette Bening.(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Blind adventurer Amar Latif travels along one of the region's most stunning river valleys, abseiling into a hidden gorge, canoeing across a reservoir, and meeting the local llamas while following a riverside trail along the Nidd.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates an unexpected place of respite for the weary traveller by painting a lonesome cabin in the middle of a snowy setting.(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Stiff Upper Lip: An Emotional History of Britain
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the British expressed their feelings throughout the 20th century and asks whether regular displays of emotion on today's TV confirm the departure of the 'stiff upper lip'. He begins with the 1926 General Strike, revealing how behaviour on both sides prevented an all-out revolution, and visits the Imperial War Museum to discover the original story behind the morale-boosting slogan Keep Calm and Carry On. He also meets playwright Alan Bennett and looks at the nation's reactions to tragedies including the 1966 Aberfan disaster and the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, in 1997.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A compilation of classic Kylie Minogue performances from the Top of the Pops studio.(n)

programme.title: Germaine Bloody Greer
programme.desc: Profile of the writer and academic, whose 1970 book The Female Eunuch became an international bestseller and an important part of the feminist movement. The programme features previously unseen archive footage, while interviews with Greer reveal with typical honesty, candour and wit her thoughts on the days when the book was originally published, as well as her opinion on the MeToo movement. Camille Paglia, Zoe Heller and Rosie Boycott are among those sharing their opinions on the influence of The Female Eunuch.(n)

programme.title: Virago: Changing the World One Page at a Time
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the origins and development of the publishing house founded in 1973 by Carmen Callil to promote books by female writers. Featuring contributions from Virago authors including Margaret Atwood, Sarah Waters, Sarah Dunant, Maya Angelou and Naomi Wolf as well as founding team members Carmen Callil, Ursula Owen, Harriet Spicer, Lennie Goodings and Alexandra Pringle.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's Great Scots: The Writers Who Shaped a Nation
programme.desc: As Scotland stands on the brink of a momentous decision on its independence from the rest of the UK, Andrew Marr explores the writers who have defined and challenged Scottish national identity over the past 300 years. He begins with 18th-century Edinburgh-born lawyer, diarist and author James Boswell, a man torn between patriotic duty at home and a desire for fame and adventure elsewhere, and whose biography of Samuel Johnson was a landmark in the development of the genre. The broadcaster retraces Boswell's journey from his privileged and austere childhood in Ayrshire to the vibrant streets of London and on to the wilderness of the Western Isles.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates an unexpected place of respite for the weary traveller by painting a lonesome cabin in the middle of a snowy setting.(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Blind adventurer Amar Latif travels along one of the region's most stunning river valleys, abseiling into a hidden gorge, canoeing across a reservoir, and meeting the local llamas while following a riverside trail along the Nidd.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Stiff Upper Lip: An Emotional History of Britain
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the British expressed their feelings throughout the 20th century and asks whether regular displays of emotion on today's TV confirm the departure of the 'stiff upper lip'. He begins with the 1926 General Strike, revealing how behaviour on both sides prevented an all-out revolution, and visits the Imperial War Museum to discover the original story behind the morale-boosting slogan Keep Calm and Carry On. He also meets playwright Alan Bennett and looks at the nation's reactions to tragedies including the 1966 Aberfan disaster and the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, in 1997.(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Shobna Gulati takes a stroll through Shropshire and Worcestershire along the banks of the Severn, Great Britain's longest river, finding out about a part of the country that is a world away from her native north-west England.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a landscape featuring all his trademarks - a lake, tall trees and a cabin with a majestic mountain in the background.(n)

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning writer Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel, first published in 1815. Romola Garai stars as the independent Emma Woodhouse, who believes she is a skilled matchmaker and tries to pair up her friends and acquaintances. She takes Harriet Smith under her wing and encourages the woman to set her sights higher than her current suitor - against Mr Knightley's warnings about the dangers of meddling. With Jonny Lee Miller, Michael Gambon, Jodhi May and Tamsin Greig.(n)

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton.(n)

programme.title: My Friend Jane
programme.desc: Documentary shedding light on the lives of Jane Austen super-fans, who read, live and breathe all-things Regency. While Austen's works are often considered genteel romances, they are often shot through with acid-sharp wit, social commentary, and a hint of biography, with certain elements from her famous novels more than mirroring aspects of Jane's own life. Here, the self-professed 'Janeites' share their thoughts on why they have fallen in love with Austen's genteel narratives, and explain what the work of a vicar's daughter from Hampshire who died 200 years ago means to them.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott investigates the strange dimming of Betelgeuse, while Maggie Aderin-Pocock reports on the recent discovery of the biggest and brightest supernova ever observed. Lucie Green discovers how historical drawings that pre-date the invention of the telescope are helping to predict solar activity, and Peter Lawrence attempts to photograph our very own star.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Star Men: Heroes of Astronomy
programme.desc: Four veteran British stargazers go on a road trip to celebrate 50 years of work and friendship, which represents a highly productive period in astronomy. Now in their 70s, they share their reflections on a life spent looking at the universe as they journey through the south-western United States, revisiting the places where they worked and the landscape they explored together as young men.(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the Royal Collection - the dazzling array of art and decorative objects collected by kings and queens over the past 500 years, and today owned by the Queen as sovereign. The first programme focuses on the works acquired by the collection's founders - Henry VIII and Charles I. Henry VIII tried to overwhelm with magnificence, believing that great art projected power. For Charles I art was a way to compete with other kings through taste, and Andrew reveals how this doomed monarch became the greatest royal collector in British history.(n)

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Art historian Helen Rosslyn opens the doors of impressive country houses to tell the stories of how so many great paintings came to Britain, and of the people who brought them here. In the first episode, she reveals the influence of the 17th-century pioneer collectors, including Thomas Howard (the Earl of Arundel) and King Charles I and his entourage known as the Whitehall Group, and explores how the latter introduced a taste for the Baroque to the country, commissioning artists such as Rubens, Van Dyke and Antonio Verrio.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a landscape featuring all his trademarks - a lake, tall trees and a cabin with a majestic mountain in the background.(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Former Coronation Street star Shobna Gulati takes a stroll through Shropshire and Worcestershire along the banks of the Severn, Great Britain's longest river, finding out about a part of the country that is a world away from her native north-west England.(n)

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton.(n)

programme.title: Nigel Kennedy at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of the musician's performances from the BBC archive, documenting the British violinist and violist's development and career, from a seven-year old child on Town and Around, to his virtuoso show-stopper Czardas from the Last Night of the Proms 2013. Featuring interviews with him through the years, the programme studies his versatility of styles, including classical, experimental and jazz.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe provides football fans with a chance to revisit some of the best (and worst) songs produced to celebrate the tournament's conclusion over the years. Ditties such as Manchester United's 1996 song Move Move Move, featuring a youthful Paul Scholes, David Beckham and the Neville brothers, could feature alongside the arguably more 'professional' sounding efforts of the London Community Gospel Choir, which sang Abide With Me - the FA Cup's traditional anthem - ahead of Chelsea's clash with Everton at Wembley in 2009.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first broadcast on 14 December, featuring performances by Rob'n'Raz, Van Morrison and Cliff Richard, Madonna, Latino Rave, Wet Wet Wet and Tina Turner.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Anthea Turner present the edition first broadcast on 21 December, featuring performances by Sonia, Bros, Band Aid II, FPI Project, Duran Duran and the Christians.(n)

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: Huey visits New York City, home to over a million Puerto Ricans and the place where he first fell in love with the hot sounds of Latin Music. 'El Barrio' or Spanish Harlem became the focal point for the community, with Latin music the soundtrack to their survival. Fusing traditional music with American R'n'B and soul, newly minted Nuyoricans created the '60s dance craze, the Boogaloo. Flying south to Puerto Rico itself Huey sets off on a journey to discover the music that was left behind, hunting down the roots of Salsa in the capital San Juan, and the traditional folk styles of Bomba and Plena.(n)

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: A performance by the world renowned Cuatro player, the Latin American guitar made famous in Luis Fonsi's hit song Despacito. The Grammy Award-winning star is joined by family and friends in the mountains of Puerto Rico to present some of his favourite music.(n)

programme.title: Roy Orbison: One of the Lonely Ones
programme.desc: Biography of the singer and musician, casting new light on the triumphs and tragedies that beset his career, featuring performances and home movies, and providing an insight into Orbison's remote Texas childhood and his battles to get his voice heard. This film reveals how his rollercoaster life was often reflected in the dark lyrics of his songs, from success to rejection, and rediscovery in the 1980s with the Travelling Wilburys supergroup. Featuring interviews with Jeff Lynne, T Bone Burnett, Bobby Goldsboro and Marianne Faithfull.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Crazy coincidences have prevented Brain from seeing a meteor shower in person. But this time, he's planned for everything. Will Brain finally satisfy his astronomical ambition…or is he cosmically cursed?(n)

With the threat of bankruptcy looming, can the council provide the support and services the locals need to get them through the next phase of the health and economic crisis?(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Scott and Gethin Jones host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report from Salford as the city lifts the lockdown and tries to get its community back to work. The programme follows the mayor and his team, looking at the burden that has fallen on local councils, and also showing the work done by teachers, bus drivers and those helping the homeless. But for Salford it has been expensive. With the threat of bankruptcy looming, can the council provide the support and services the locals need to get them through the next phase of the health and economic crisis?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Last night saw actress Felicity Montagu, singer Lady Leshurr and presenters Amar Latif, Gethin Jones and Jeff Brazier enter the fray. But one of them has already been voted out, leaving the other four to cook on. They begin with the pairs task, for which they are split into teams and have to serve up identical dishes - all while separated by a wall. The second challenge sees them entering the world of mass catering at the Wellcome Genome Campus in Cambridgeshire.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary following the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The control room experiences a spike in calls for patients with breathing difficulties, and when Sherilee talks to a daughter calls whose mother has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, it proves tough for the call handler as her own mum had the same illness. Meanwhile, call handler Brogan has to guide a caller through lifesaving CPR while they wait for an ambulance, and there's a full emergency at John Lennon Airport when a plane loses contact with air traffic control.(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season.(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists to make something special for the homes of people they've never met. In Lincolnshire, a buyer is looking for a gift for her husband as a thank-you for all he does for the family and to mark his long service to charity work. Will a stone sculptor, a mosaic maker or an illustrator create the perfect present? Presented by Nick Knowles.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Fed up with Cathy festering in her bed, post-wedding cancellation, and stalking Marcus and Meredith on what was supposed to be Cathy and Marcus' honeymoon, Cat decides the best thing to cheer up her half-sibling is to take her away on their own 'sister honeymoon'. She's got just the place in mind - their late Dad's favourite pub. At the solicitors, Marilyn and Tess learn that Colin's will contains an absolute bombshell and that they must pass on some shocking news to their daughters. Comedy, starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Siobhan Finneran and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Kayleigh has had enough of work and fancies a day off, but John is not having any of it - or is he? Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride.(n)

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: Documentary series following the stories of Northern Ireland's country music enthusiasts. This edition focuses on Ireland's biggest truck show, meeting the lorry-loving women who spend hundreds of thousands on their vehicles to keep them in tip-top condition, and the men who combine their weekly workload with pulling up to country music festivals at the weekends.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Fed up with Cathy festering in her bed, post-wedding cancellation, and stalking Marcus and Meredith on what was supposed to be Cathy and Marcus' honeymoon, Cat decides the best thing to cheer up her half-sibling is to take her away on their own 'sister honeymoon'. She's got just the place in mind - their late Dad's favourite pub. At the solicitors, Marilyn and Tess learn that Colin's will contains an absolute bombshell and that they must pass on some shocking news to their daughters. Comedy, starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Siobhan Finneran and Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: Olympics 2012
programme.desc: A look back at the show produced by Trainspotting and Slumdog Millionaire director Danny Boyle, which marked the start of the London 2012 Olympic Games. The ceremony reflected the key themes and priorities of the event, based on sport, inspiration, youth and urban transformation, and celebrated the contributions made by the UK to the world through innovation, as well as the creativity of British people.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Children seem largely unaffected by physical aspects of the coronavirus. But as the lockdown eases, concerns are growing about young people's mental health as a result of the crisis. Julie Etchingham asks what life has been like for the younger generation, and as the costs are counted - with thousands of teaching hours lost, exams missed and the country entering the worst recession on record - will it have any negative impact on their ambitions and prospects?(n)

programme.title: Stephen Lawrence: Has Britain Changed?
programme.desc: The killing of George Floyd in the US triggered global protests, with thousands also taking to the streets in Britain. In light of these events, Rageh Omaar and Anushka Asthana host this live debate to examine racial equality in the UK. They discuss how much has changed in Britain almost three decades after the racist murder of teenager Stephen Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: The Murder of Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: Director Paul Greengrass's 1999 dramatisation of Doreen and Neville Lawrence's struggle to bring their 18-year-old son's killers to justice, exposing the flaws in the system that had failed them. Marianne Jean-Baptiste and Hugh Quarshie star as the bereaved parents, with David Calder as Metropolitan Police Chief Sir Paul Condon. Kenneth Cranham and Michael Feast also star. It won Best Single Drama at the 2000 Bafta Awards.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Rhona try to calm Cain as Kim threatens to bring down Butlers Farm, while Jamie feels trapped as Andrea holds all the cards. Nicola slyly railroads Jimmy into suggesting that they buy into the cafe, and Belle's heart is broken as her hopes are crushed.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin asks Abi to move in with him and Jack, but, while she is delighted, Seb is angry that she has moved on so quickly from her own kids to Kevin's. Geoff promises Yasmeen that when she comes home, things will be different and he'll seek professional help, while the factory girls are shocked when they realise Carla is once more their boss.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Barry Gibb reflects on his life and 50-year career as a songwriter and singer, explaining how he has finally managed to come to terms with being the last surviving member of the Bee Gees. The musician recalls how the group rose from child performers to global superstars in the wake of the Saturday Night Fever soundtrack in the late 1970s. He also reveals how a childhood accident nearly killed him, what really happened when he and his future wife sneaked into Dr Who's Tardis, and gives his verdict when Piers serenades him with his falsetto voice.(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Rottweiler, Ladybug, Tree, Penguin, Flower and Fox take to the stage, while panellists Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, and regular star of the British spin-off Ken Jeong try to work out who is behind the mask.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part one of two. Angry and resentful at being left behind while his parents set sail on a luxury cruise, Stewie arms himself with a machine gun and gives chase in a speedboat, intent on murdering Lois.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's luck as a writer changes dramatically when Stewie becomes his manager, and one of his books hits the bestseller list. However, fame soon goes to his head, and it takes a wake-up call from satirist Bill Maher to bring him to his senses. Comedy, featuring the voices of Mila Kunis and Seth Green.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois befriends local news presenter Joyce Kinney, who asks for permission to film a feature depicting her as the model American housewife. However, the report ends up making her look like a fool, so she sets out to put things right.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A mishap at home with charity drive chocolates lands Steve in trouble at school, and he is placed in the 'At-Risk' class with the student body's worst trouble-makers.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: In his continuing quest to meet girls, Steve decides to try to earn a letter jacket by joining the school's water polo team. When his initial attempt fails, he enlists Klaus' help to make the squad, and soon becomes the team's star player. Meanwhile, Stan and Roger buy a sailboat, but find life on water is not so easy as they imagined.(n)

programme.title: Timewasters
programme.desc: Nick opens his very own jazz club, but Lauren has bigger plans for the place, while a mysterious woman called Janice is tailing the gang.(n)

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling hosts as five friends take on Scarlett Moffatt, Samira Mighty, Tom Parker, London Hughes and Brennan Reece in challenges based on unusual abilities.(n)

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Joey Essex, Charlotte Crosby and Pete Bennett take part in a celebrity version of the horror game show, where they enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges during which they must unlock chests full of prize money. In order to win the jackpot they need to get back out again, but the cash is protected by a pack of angry dogs. Hosted by Reggie Yates.(n)

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: Liz is forced to revisit a painful part of her past when her mother shows up at the diner unexpectedly. Michael urges Maria to seek help after she experiences a strange vision, while Kyle's attempt to get Steph to open up doesn't go as planned.(n)

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Iain Stirling celebrates some of the memorable characters who have called the villa home and made viewers cry with laughter in the process. From questionable rapping to secret societies and unique views on politics, he introduces clips illustrating the most bonkers behaviour from the past six series.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Brian are forced to attend a 12-step AA meeting to kick their drinking habits following a public disturbance. While the dog decides to mend his ways, Peter refuses to admit he has a problem, prompting Death to intervene and show him what his life would be like without alcohol.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Mayor West marries Lois's sister and the pair move into the Griffin family home, where he becomes Peter's best friend. However, not everyone welcomes the new arrivals, and Stewie takes over Chris's room after being evicted from his own.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim tells a horrified Andy and Anne that he dumped Liz on a roadside. Jack and Vera are alarmed to find Roy camping in their cellar.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy and Steve are ashamed of their dad and do not want anyone knowing he hit Liz. Maxine is upset when Fiona decides to move out.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff starts posting off job applications. Frank puts in his offer for Emmerdale Farm in writing. Alan suspects that the Dingles stole some beer but cannot prove it was them.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank does not want the police investigating the explosion at the quarry. Annie returns from Spain with shocking news for Jack. The Dingles prepare to do battle with the protestors.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Tension mounts as explosives go missing from Lord Ashfordly's quarry just before the Defence Minister is due to arrive on an official visit to Fylingdales early warning station, prompting Blaketon to embark on an off-the-record investigation. Meanwhile, Dr Alway makes the mistake of enlisting Vernon and David as bellringers - will the duo drop a clanger?(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike bring home a tramp, much to Sid's disgust. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious road accident causes chaos for the residents of Aidensfield when a tanker containing a mysterious substance is breached, leaving a reservoir polluted and Ashfordly police tearing their hair out as they try to track down the culprit. Luckily, the staff of nearby St Aidan's Royal Free hospital are on hand to offer some much-needed assistance.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris smugly tells Frank how Annie has forced Jack into selling Emmerdale Farm. However, the Sugdens receive a better offer. Frank is unconvinced by Kim's new maternal instincts.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah and Jack fall out over the sale of the farm. Tina is furious with Steve for going behind Frank's back. A heart-to-heart between Kathy and Dave leads to a surprise.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim tells Andy about Liz's affair with Johnny. Liz talks to a domestic violence officer, and then tells Jim their marriage is over. Mike takes out a mortgage on the flat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel starts acting secretively, Steve and Andy are saddened by the news of Liz divorcing Jim, and Mike talks Alma into signing over her share of the flat.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious road accident causes chaos for the residents of Aidensfield when a tanker containing a mysterious substance is breached, leaving a reservoir polluted and Ashfordly police tearing their hair out as they try to track down the culprit. Luckily, the staff of nearby St Aidan's Royal Free hospital are on hand to offer some much-needed assistance.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It's the early autumn of 1924 and Rose's father-in-law Lord Sinderby has rented Brancaster Castle in Northumberland and invited the Crawleys to a grouse-shooting party. However, the Sinderbys' butler Stowell (guest star Alun Armstrong, New Tricks) has an axe to grind and a scandalous secret threatens to derail the holiday. Back at Downton, the servants hold the fort and unlikely later-life romances abound, while Bates takes drastic measures to clear Anna's name as she faces an uncertain future behind bars. The seasons change and another Christmas is enjoyed at the Abbey, where a heartbreaking farewell and a joyful reunion take place. Feature-length festive special, starring Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, James Faulkner, Penny Downie, Joanne Froggatt and Brendan Coyle.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films).(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval).(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law.(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: An evening of watching telly is ruined when the set breaks down _ but worse is yet to come.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress thinks Mike's replacement PC Steve Crane is too anxious to prove his worth as he investigates a spate of farm break-ins on the moors, and Vernon causes chaos trying to impress new arrival Dr Liz Merrick with his community spirit. Elsewhere, Mike grows disillusioned with CID work. James Carlton and Aislin McGuckin star as the newcomers.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris tells Rachel he wants her back. Kim pressures Dave into going for a drink with her. Vic plays a practical joke on Viv and it explosively backfires.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv feels humiliated by Vic, Jack's pessimism frustrates Sarah when they look for a new farm to buy, and Rachel makes a fool of herself when she attempts to seduce Steve.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy and Steve urge Liz to change her mind about divorcing Jim. Liz asks Des to give her a job. Joyce is intrigued to discover Vera's son is in prison.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre hopes Mike will give her a job in his factory. Sean grows interested in Liz when he hears she has separated from Jim. Andy challenges his mum over her relationship with Des.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress thinks Mike's replacement PC Steve Crane is too anxious to prove his worth as he investigates a spate of farm break-ins on the moors, and Vernon causes chaos trying to impress new arrival Dr Liz Merrick with his community spirit. Elsewhere, Mike grows disillusioned with CID work. James Carlton and Aislin McGuckin star as the newcomers.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen.(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Three weeks after reporting her husband missing, adult-film star Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of his murder. Rachel faces the task of going through the suspect's home-made pornography collection in search of clues, but the case takes a dark twist when Vicky's daughter turns up with her much older boyfriend. Meanwhile, Janet's affair with a colleague is exposed. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is embarrassed by his behaviour towards Tina. Biff wants to know why Dave is rushing into marriage. The Dingles hope to clean up selling Christmas stock in the village.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not happy in his role as Santa, Kathy and Dave's wedding day arrives, and Vic catches Viv and Terry sharing a romantic moment together.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie applies for a job as office manager with Mike. Andy tries to get Steve to see that Liz as much to blame as Jim. Fiona throws a grand opening party for her salon.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim returns from Ireland determined to put things right with Liz, but she refuses to see him. Maureen is annoyed when Reg calls off visiting her at the weekend.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP struggles to come to terms with his new role as a father, having planned to resume his career as a consultant in London before the arrival of his and Louisa's as-yet unnamed son. To make matters worse, he begins to have serious doubts about the competency of his replacement Dr Di Dibbs when she misdiagnoses a patient as well as herself. Martin is then dealt a devastating personal blow after hearing his aunt Joan has died. Starring Martin Clunes, Joanna Scanlan and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels.(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves.(n)

Meanwhile, Dr Alway makes the mistake of enlisting Vernon and David as bellringers - will the duo drop a clanger?(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike bring home a tramp, much to Sid's disgust. Comedy, starring Sid James.(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A serious road accident causes chaos for the residents of Aidensfield when a tanker containing a mysterious substance is breached, leaving a reservoir polluted and Ashfordly police tearing their hair out as they try to track down the culprit. Luckily, the staff of nearby St Aidan's Royal Free hospital are on hand to offer some much-needed assistance.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
