programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It's the early autumn of 1924 and Rose's father-in-law Lord Sinderby has rented Brancaster Castle in Northumberland and invited the Crawleys to a grouse-shooting party. However, the Sinderbys' butler Stowell (guest star Alun Armstrong, New Tricks) has an axe to grind and a scandalous secret threatens to derail the holiday. Back at Downton, the servants hold the fort and unlikely later-life romances abound, while Bates takes drastic measures to clear Anna's name as she faces an uncertain future behind bars. The seasons change and another Christmas is enjoyed at the Abbey, where a heartbreaking farewell and a joyful reunion take place. Feature-length festive special, starring Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, James Faulkner, Penny Downie, Joanne Froggatt and Brendan Coyle.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films).(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval).(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

Elsewhere, Mike grows disillusioned with CID work. James Carlton and Aislin McGuckin star as the newcomers.(n)

programme.desc: Chris tells Rachel he wants her back. Kim pressures Dave into going for a drink with her. Vic plays a practical joke on Viv and it explosively backfires.(n)

programme.desc: Viv feels humiliated by Vic, Jack's pessimism frustrates Sarah when they look for a new farm to buy, and Rachel makes a fool of herself when she attempts to seduce Steve.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen.(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Three weeks after reporting her husband missing, adult-film star Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of his murder. Rachel faces the task of going through the suspect's home-made pornography collection in search of clues, but the case takes a dark twist when Vicky's daughter turns up with her much older boyfriend. Meanwhile, Janet's affair with a colleague is exposed. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is embarrassed by his behaviour towards Tina. Biff wants to know why Dave is rushing into marriage. The Dingles hope to clean up selling Christmas stock in the village.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not happy in his role as Santa, Kathy and Dave's wedding day arrives, and Vic catches Viv and Terry sharing a romantic moment together.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie applies for a job as office manager with Mike. Andy tries to get Steve to see that Liz as much to blame as Jim. Fiona throws a grand opening party for her salon.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim returns from Ireland determined to put things right with Liz, but she refuses to see him. Maureen is annoyed when Reg calls off visiting her at the weekend.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP struggles to come to terms with his new role as a father, having planned to resume his career as a consultant in London before the arrival of his and Louisa's as-yet unnamed son. To make matters worse, he begins to have serious doubts about the competency of his replacement Dr Di Dibbs when she misdiagnoses a patient as well as herself. Martin is then dealt a devastating personal blow after hearing his aunt Joan has died. Starring Martin Clunes, Joanna Scanlan and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels.(n)

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Archie and Figgis are feeling the ill-effects of their smoking habit and resolve to quit. However, they find it tougher than imagined as Norman has just taken up the pastime. Starring Peter Bowles and James Bolam.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Nightclub owner Paul Verrier is tricked into believing that the Saint is trying to kill him, so contacts various people to murder Templer. One of his nefarious contacts is unknown to him visually, so when the Saint eventually pays a call Verrier mistakes him for his hired assassin and inadvertently hires the Saint to kill himself. Roger Moore stars.(n)

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Conclusion. Robin and his outlaw band storm the evil Baron de Belleme's stronghold in a bid to rescue Lady Marion and retrieve the silver arrow. Michael Praed, Anthony Valentine and Judi Trott star.(n)

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A hijacked security van mysteriously disappears, and the stolen loot leaves no clues for the mismatched duo. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A criminal escapes from Chelmsford prison protesting his innocence and hoping to get the media's help to secure a release date - so Arthur tries to make money by selling his exclusive story to the highest bidder in Fleet Street. George Cole and John Labanowski star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle spring into action when East German agents blackmail a government employee into revealing secret plans for a new type of jet-engine tank. Featuring an appearance by playwright Lynda La Plante, alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A sergeant is injured in a shooting incident and names his informant as the person responsible. However, the suspected man's girlfriend insists that the officer is corrupt and steals away to be with her lover, who has gone into hiding. Lynda La Plante guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Counterfeiters lead Simon a merry dance from London to Switzerland via Paris, and the involvement of a bogus countess only complicates matters further. Roger Moore stars, with Kate O'Mara and Philip Madoc.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a CIA agent dies after being thrown off a balcony, the unit sets up a fake arms deal with the German terrorist cell responsible. However, the gunrunners uncover the ploy, kidnap Bodie and transform him into a walking time-bomb. Lewis Collins stars.(n)

programme.title: Crimson Tide (1995)
programme.desc: A veteran US submarine captain receives an incomplete message after a Russian base falls into rebel hands, and is convinced it is an order to fire his nuclear weapons. However, his first officer refuses to take such potentially devastating action without confirmed orders, leading to a deadly battle of wills. Thriller, starring Gene Hackman, Denzel Washington and Viggo Mortensen.(n)

The trip takes them to Britain, France and Germany, causing mayhem wherever they go, while the final destination in Rome leads to a run-in with a gang of thieves. Comedy sequel, starring Chevy Chase and Beverly D'Angelo.(n)

The first episode examines the case of a teenager cheated out of her grandfather's inheritance by her own dad and stepmother. There is also the story of a pair of identical twins who fell out over a £55,000 loan, and a man who was alleged to have robbed his mother's pension and life savings, but took his own life before justice was done.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris sets out on a mission to cross three former Soviet republics entirely by rail in just one week, travelling through Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania and uncovering amazing stories and dark secrets from the region's past. He begins in Narva, Estonia, before heading to the capital Tallinn on the country's oldest railway, built by German nobles to connect the Russian imperial capital St Petersburg with Estonia's ports, then travelling through Latvia and Lithuania and heading to the Russian border on a revolutionary new line.(n)

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the river, and how it has played a pivotal role in London's development as a major centre for international trade. This episode focuses on the capital's docklands, which at their peak, employed more than 30,000 workers. A boat captain whose family have hauled cargo along the river for six generations recalls his grandfather's unique talents. Plus, a look at the construction of bridges, sewers, and the flood barrier.(n)

programme.title: The Hunt for Cleopatra's Tomb
programme.desc: Egyptologist Glenn Godenho explores the groundbreaking work of archaeologist Kathleen Martinez, who is on a quest to find the burial site of Cleopatra. Egypt's last pharaoh died in 30 BC, but the location of her tomb remains a mystery and Dr Martinez has dedicated her research to finding it, believing it to be at the site of the ancient temple of Taposiris Magna, just west of Alexandria.(n)

programme.title: 10 Mistakes That Sank the Titanic
programme.desc: Documentary examining the chain of events that led to the maritime disaster in April 1912, when the ship struck an iceberg in the north Atlantic and went down with around 1,500 passengers and crew. From the procedure for fixing rivets into the ship's bows, the delays in its construction that pushed the maiden voyage into perilous iceberg season, and the astonishing fact that the lookouts on watch that fateful night had no access to binoculars, this film reveals the 10 key mistakes that conspired to sink the supposedly unsinkable Titanic.(n)

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: Documentary using visual effects to 'drain' the waters from around Alcatraz island, which reveals San Francisco Bay to be a fascinating and chaotic place. On the dry bay floor are scars left by earthquakes, giving scientists an idea of when the next one will strike - and what could happen to San Francisco if one does. Not only that - the draining also reveals why the notorious Alcatraz island prison, which sat in the south of the bay, proved so inescapable.(n)

programme.title: When Buildings Collapse: World's Worst Engineering Disasters
programme.desc: Rob Bell investigates some terrible engineering disasters. In 2018, a Miami bridge collapsed killed six people, leaving investigators wondering what turned the $11million structure into a lethal object. Six months later, 43 people were killed when a bridge in Genoa also failed. Back in 1981, the atrium of a new hotel in Kansas City crashed to the ground, costing over 100 lives. Rob discovers how a change in the design of the walkways was responsible. Despite the sad fact that design flaws and construction errors cost lives, Rob reveals how such occurrences also teach engineers how to save them.(n)

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling.(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben heads to the remote Wilderness Island, off Australia's north-western cape, to meet a man whose story is as remarkable as the landscape he inhabits. He also gets a lesson in the art of mud-crabbing and an understanding of the urgent need for fresh water, which the island is currently without.(n)

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas presents a whistle-stop tour of awkward, embarrassing and cringeworthy holiday moments, as related by a selection of comedians and celebrities. Johnny's famous contributors skewer the traditional staycation, questioning the perks of rain-drenched British beaches, and whether anyone really likes going camping. Turning their attention further afield, the stars share some of their most memorable holiday tales, including transforming a hotel corridor into one long 'slip-and-slide', the suspicious discovery of a pair of chicken fillets in a swimming pool in Marbella, and a blush-inducing incident involving a naked paragliding session along a Spanish beachfront.(n)

programme.title: Wildlife SOS

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow profiles scientist, engineer, inventor and painter Leonardo da Vinci, whose extraordinary artistic genius gave rise to masterpieces including the Mona Lisa, the Annunciation and the Last Supper. Breaking new ground in portraiture and historical imagery, this giant of the Italian Renaissance used his wide-ranging skills to make a unique contribution to the development of European art.(n)

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley goes on a seaside break with an inexpensive DSLR camera to see if it can capture the essence of a great British holiday, while Craig Charles learns to play the piano with help from a smart speaker. Ortis Deley goes behind-the-scenes at a leading e-sports team's training camp to find out how these new stars of the gaming world prepare for a major event. Plus, the G-Team dusts itself down to find the cleaning gadgets that promise to make household chores a breeze and Jordan Erica Webber dives into the world of gaming.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a trip through Wales' highest peaks and deepest valleys. Starting in Pwllheli in the north west, the route heads along the lines that were built during the slate industry's heyday. At Porthmadog, the journey briefly switches to the reconstructed steam trains of the Welsh Highland National Heritage Railway to Minffordd station. Nearby is the village of Portmeirion, famous for its role in the classic TV series The Prisoner. Rejoining the Cambrian Line, local farmers reveal what the railway means to them, before the route takes in the seaside resort of Aberdovey.(n)

programme.title: A Greek Odyssey with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: The historian travels through the Corinth Canal - a feat of engineering that required the removal of 12 million cubic metres of earth - before heading for her penultimate stop of Corfu, where Count Flamburiari reveals the island's close connections to Britain. From there, Bettany sails to Ithaca, Odysseus's home island, finally completing her 1,700-mile voyage across the Greek Islands. However, there's a startling surprise in store when she is awoken in the night by an earthquake measuring 4.9 on the Richter scale.(n)

programme.title: Love Actually (2003)
programme.desc: Ten interlinked stories of people searching for and finding love. While the newly elected, bachelor British prime minister is smitten by a junior staff member at 10 Downing Street, a heartbroken writer heads for France to get over a failed relationship, and a faded rock star aims to reinvent himself in time for Christmas. Richard Curtis's romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Martine McCutcheon, Emma Thompson, Alan Rickman, Colin Firth, Bill Nighy and Liam Neeson.(n)

Western, starring James Stewart, Audie Murphy, Dan Duryea and Brandon de Wilde.(n)

programme.title: Freedom Radio (1940)
programme.desc: A Viennese doctor tries to maintain business as usual while living under Nazi rule. However, events take a sickening turn when his wife begins wholeheartedly espousing Hitler's views - prompting him to start broadcasting Allied propaganda from a secret transmitter. Second World War drama, starring Clive Brook, Diana Wynyard, Raymond Huntley and Derek Farr.(n)

programme.title: Gun Fury (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy rancher and his fiancee take the stagecoach to California, where they intend to start a new life in the wake of the Civil War. However, during the journey an ex-Confederate outlaw and his gang of thugs attack the coach and kidnap the bride-to-be. Distraught and wounded, the rancher sets out to rescue her and punish her abductors. Western, with Rock Hudson, Donna Reed, Lee Marvin and Phil Carey.(n)

programme.title: Gallipoli (1981)
programme.desc: Two patriotic Australians join the army to fight during the First World War. However, their youthful idealism is quickly eroded by the harsh reality of the conflict, and their friendship is tested to the very limits of endurance as they prepare for combat ahead of the pivotal battle of Gallipoli in Turkey. Drama, directed by Peter Weir, starring Mel Gibson, Mark Lee, Bill Kerr and David Argue.(n)

programme.title: Star Trek (2009)
programme.desc: The trainee crew members of the starship Enterprise embark on their maiden flight, which pits them against a Romulan commander from the future who is planning a horrific revenge on those he blames for a tragedy in his own time. Sci-fi adventure based on the original TV series, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto, Eric Bana, Zoe Saldana and Leonard Nimoy.(n)

programme.title: War for the Planet of the Apes (2017)
programme.desc: A devastating conflict erupts between humans and intelligent primates in a post-apocalyptic future. The leader of the apes is determined to make peace and prove his people are not savages - but a tragic course of events leaves him wrestling with a desire for revenge. Sci-fi adventure sequel, the conclusion of the trilogy of prequels to the 1968 movie. Starring Andy Serkis, Woody Harrelson and Steve Zahn.(n)

programme.title: The Gunman (2015)
programme.desc: A mercenary goes into hiding after successfully carrying out a hit on an African politician that sparks a series of uprisings and violent acts. He re-emerges years later, only to become the latest target of his former colleagues. Crime drama, starring Sean Penn, Idris Elba, Ray Winstone, Javier Bardem and Mark Rylance.(n)

programme.title: Nico, 1988 (2017)
programme.desc: Biopic about the German singer who found fame with the Velvet Underground in the 1960s, following her on tour with her band in the 1980s as she struggles with heroin addiction. Starring Trine Dyrholm, with John Gordon Sinclair and Anamaria Marinca.(n)

programme.title: The Fighting Kentuckian (1949)
programme.desc: French settlers are granted asylum in America in the aftermath of Napoleon's defeat at Waterloo and a Kentucky rifleman finds himself falling for one of the newcomers - a general's daughter. However, he faces a rival for her affections in the form of a ruthless landowner who plans to steal the terrritory her fellow exiles have settled on. George Waggner's Western, starring John Wayne, Vera Hruba Ralston, Philip Dorn and Oliver Hardy.(n)

programme.title: Appointment with Danger (1950)
programme.desc: A nun is targeted by two murderers when they realise she is the only witness to one of their killings, and it is up to postal inspector to protect her and bring the men to justice. Noir thriller, starring Phyllis Calvert, Alan Ladd and Paul Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Comanche Station (1960)
programme.desc: A cowboy recruits a trio of outlaws to rescue a woman held captive at a Comanche camp and reunite her with her husband in return for a large reward. However, his partners decide the bounty should be split three ways rather than four and plan to double-cross him. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Nancy Gates, Claude Akins and Skip Homeier.(n)

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin (1966)
programme.desc: Former crook and disreputable spy Harry Palmer has to carefully engineer the defection of a Russian officer responsible for safeguarding vital military secrets - but the mission proves less than straightforward. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deighton's character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman.(n)

programme.title: Ender's Game (2013)
programme.desc: In the future, the human race has survived conflict with an alien race, but the prospect of war breaking out again is a constant threat. A gifted boy is selected as a potential military commander for the coming conflict, and subjected to a gruelling training program to prove his worth. Sci-fi adventure based on Orson Scott Card's novel, starring Asa Butterfield, Harrison Ford and Ben Kingsley.(n)

programme.title: Kingsman: The Secret Service (2015)
programme.desc: A streetwise teenager is taken under the wing of his dead father's old colleague, an agent working for a super-secret spy organisation. Will the youngster prove to be a chip off the old block by proving useful during a battle against a twisted, hi-tech genius? Action comedy based on a comic book, starring Colin Firth, Taron Egerton and Samuel L Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Escape Plan (2013)
programme.desc: An expert takes a contract from the CIA to test a maximum security prison by trying to break out of it, but he soon discovers he has been duped and that his captors intend him to stay inside for ever. He hatches a plan to escape, but to succeed he will need the help of a long-term convict - who wants his own freedom in return for assistance. Thriller, with Sylvester Stallone and Arnold Schwarzenegger.(n)

programme.title: Anomalisa (2015)
programme.desc: A motivational speaker beaten down by his failing marriage and dull routine checks into a hotel as her prepares to attend a conference. He finds himself drawn to a lonely woman, and their chance encounter helps him to get to grips with his existential despair. Stop-motion animated drama, with the voices of David Thewlis, Jennifer Jason Leigh and Tom Noonan.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals (1966)
programme.desc: A wealthy Texan hires four mercenaries to rescue his wife, who has been abducted and taken to Mexico by a bandit gang. However, when they find her, she refuses to leave and they have to take her by force - ending in a cross-border chase with the outlaws on their trail. Western adventure, with Burt Lancaster, Lee Marvin, Robert Ryan, Woody Strode, Jack Palance and Claudia Cardinale.(n)

programme.title: Hell Below Zero (1954)
programme.desc: A thrill-seeking explorer joins a whaling ship and is persuaded by the female captain to investigate the disappearance of her father on an earlier expedition. The pair become suspicious of her former lover - and are proved right when he leaves them for dead in Antarctic waters. Action adventure, starring Alan Ladd, Joan Tetzel, Basil Sydney and Stanley Baker.(n)

programme.title: The Last Frontier (1955)
programme.desc: A backwoods trapper signs up as a cavalry scout at a fort manned by inexperienced soldiers, where he falls in love with a colonel's wife. On taking over command of the garrison, the officer plans to lead his men in a reckless attack on a hostile Indian tribe - and it is down to the frontiersman to save his comrades from a massacre. Western, starring Victor Mature, Robert Preston and Anne Bancroft.(n)

programme.title: The Two Faces of January (2014)
programme.desc: A conman masquerades as a tour guide in Greece, and selects a wealthy American tourist and his younger wife as likely targets. However, when a murder is committed at the couple's hotel, all three are incriminated, and a dangerous alliance develops. Thriller based on Patricia Highsmith's novel, starring Viggo Mortensen, Kirsten Dunst and Oscar Isaac.(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Four (2015)
programme.desc: A gifted young scientist joins a secret project researching inter-dimensional travel, and they return from a journey to another world having been granted superhuman powers. Their abilities make them the only hope for Earth's survival when a colleague who was lost on their expedition returns to wreak vengeance. Superhero adventure, starring Miles Teller, Michael B Jordan, Kate Mara and Jamie Bell.(n)

programme.desc: Audrey decides she cannot stand Jeff's snoring any longer and orders him to sleep in another room - but he quickly takes advantage of the situation and starts to live like a college student. Comedy, starring David Spade.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ian and Elle Betchley, owners of the George and Dragon Hotel in Princes Risborough, Buckinghamshire, welcome the contestants to their B&B, but some of them are not impressed to discover a stained teapot and dirty rooms. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Drive It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Meanwhile, 46-year-old Anthony arrives in A&E after suffering chest pains during a day out at the races. Rick explores a city of many identities, boasting a young student population apparently hungry for dishes that reflect the freshest of seafood. Back home, he prepares veal and aubergine stew after meeting the doyenne of Greek cooking, Vafa Alexiadou.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey following George Bradshaw's footsteps, as the third leg of his trip from Preston to Kirkcaldy sees him embarking on a two-day tour of the Lake District. He begins in Windermere, where he travels by steamboat to the north of the lake, and continues to Claife viewing station, where he learns about the water pollution caused by increasing levels of tourism. He spends the night in Grasmere, and the following day reaches Kendal, where he buys local delicacies and meets a farmer who explains the impact of railways on farming practices.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Carlisle, where he meets a descendant of the Border Reivers, a group of ancient lawless clansmen mentioned in Victorian train enthusiast George Bradshaw's book. From there, he travels to Gretna Green to learn about the village's famous marriage trade, explores an old Ministry of Defence munitions site, and visits the centre of Glasgow, which is enjoying a revival in popularity.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Pembroke Castle in South Wales, where objects brought in to be valued include a collection of intimate photographs charting the last days of the imprisoned Russian royal family in 1917, and a stick pin presented to one of George V's servants. A dairy maid demonstrates how to use a butter maker with a connection to Queen Victoria.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world.(n)

programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how fleas are able to jump great distances for their size and attempts to discover how fast a cheetah can run.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo leaves Glasgow and travels to Carluke in Lanarkshire's Clyde Valley, where he meets Tom Clelland, who is trying to revive his family's fruit-growing business. On his way to Edinburgh, he learns about the vast range of items left on trains, before spending the night in the city's famous Balmoral Hotel. His journey ends in Kirkcaldy, where he explores the paintings donated to the local gallery by his grandfather John Blyth.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along Brunel's Great Western Railway, learning how mass tourism started in Swindon, before visiting Bath to discover the history of the famous spa baths. His last stop is Bristol, where he tries his hand at blowing blue glass.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida.(n)

programme.desc: Fletch discovers fellow inmates at Slade Prison are planning an escape over Christmas - and is so convinced the bid is doomed to fail that he takes refuge in the hospital so he cannot be blamed when the tunnel is found. programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey from Swindon to Penzance. Starting in Yatton, he travels along a line that was once used to transport fresh strawberries across the country, but was closed by Dr Beeching in the 1960s. He also visits the Gough Cave in Cheddar Gorge to view the remains of the 9,000-year-old Cheddar Man - the oldest complete skeleton in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On his journey from Swindon to Penzance, Michael Portillo explores Torquay, where he meets meteorologist David Braine to find out whether the town is Britain's warmest spot. He then takes a trip on the Paignton and Dartmouth Steam Railway, and spends the night at the Royal Castle Hotel in Dartmouth, where he learns about the town's naval past. After sailing up the River Dart he arrives in Totnes, and discovers the community's efforts to become more environmentally friendly.(n)

In Mevagissey he discovers how the methods for pilchard fishing have changed over the years, and on a visit to the Lost Gardens of Heligan, Michael learns about the 1,000-acre estate's history.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo undertakes the last leg of his journey from Swindon to Penzance. He travels to Perran Sands, where he searches for the lost church of St Piran, a place believed to be one of the earliest Christian sites in England. In Redruth, he explores the remains of the Cornish tin industry, before heading to St Ives, where he spends the night in Treganna Castle. The former politician finishes his trip in Penzance, discovering how railways link remote communities to the rest of the country.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Solar System

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall

On the northern Jurassic coast, adventurers Mark Bullock and George Malkin take on the climb of Black Church Rock, while surfing pioneer Gwyn Haslock gives a tour while preparing for the World Belly Board Championships.(n)

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: Documentary following West Midlands Ambulance Service at the peak of the Covid-19 pandemic in April, from the perspective of the crews, control room staff and managers as they deal with the human cost of the biggest ever challenge to the service. To get more ambulances onto the frontline, managers deploy student paramedics, with 21-year-old Sam and 20-year-old Caitlyn encountering patients with breathing difficulties, in care homes, and with mental health issues that appear isolation-related.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. She is joined by 25-year-old estate agent Hollie-Anna from Margate and 29-year-old Jaison from Norwich, a transsexual man who feels like he has exhausted his local dating pool. They will both choose a date after seeing what their potential partners have to offer on a purely physical level. But will they prove to be a good match?(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Mark and Candida Diacono, who have set about building a home in the shape of a plough on their 17-acre smallholding in Devon. With its complex curved roof, timber-clad exterior and steel-framed working barn, the ambitious project presents a unique technical challenge for a local builder and his team of employees, and the task proves even more intimidating when considering Mark and Candida hope to complete work on the build within seven months.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A look back at some of Noel Fitzpatrick's most memorable cases including 18-month-old chihuahua Darcy, who suffered a crushed pelvis after being hit by a car, rendering her unable to walk. X-rays revealed at least seven fractures, meaning Noel had to operate on a microscopic scale. The programme also features three-legged cat Jersey, whose sole back leg was taking so much strain that he needed knee surgery, and deerhound Willow, suffering from a suspected broken neck after a horrific accident.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon Garner and son Shaun's next customers are couple Geoff and Anita, who are hoping to realise Geoff's lifelong ambition of retiring to Spain. After nearly 40 years of marriage, living by the coast in Southend-on-Sea, they're looking for a change of scenery, with a budget of 200,000 euros. In Alicante, husband and wife team Sophie and Damon have their work cut out finding an apartment for Yorkshire couple Graham and Lisa. Not only do the properties have to tick every box for their eight-year-old son, Jamie, they also have to contend with a rival bidder for their dream villa. Sophie and Damon also have starring roles in a promo video, as Manuel aims to promote the business with his directorial debut.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The penultimate evening from the Midlands sees the second taste of the Caribbean in as many days - but host Lorna is convinced her take is the real deal.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Hull, image-conscious Jay and Heather kick off the competition with a menu inspired by their love of American food. Day two sees thrifty home birds Mike and Stef host a family favourites evening, but will everyone appreciate their school dinner throwback? Finally it's straight-talking Steve and Stephanie's chance to show how fine dining should be done.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: It's the moment of truth as harvest time arrives for olive farmers in Spain, while plumbing problems threaten the launch of a tea room in France. A Teesside family finds ways to cope when there's a fiesta in Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Mariam and Nasser, from the West Midlands, find a family holiday home they can eventually retire to in the countryside of Murcia, south-east Spain. The couple used to live near Madrid and desperately miss the Spanish sunshine and way of life, especially the outdoor cooking. Laura has five sizzling hot properties to show them for their £70,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Lea and Tony Desborough from Essex have fallen in love with Menorca on countless holidays over the last 20 years, and have dreamed of owning their own place there. Now, as they wind down their work commitments, they've decided it's time to make that dream a reality. Laura Hamilton shows them five costal villas for their £250,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Mark from the Bowling Green Hotel questions Martin and Petra about a lack of chairs and then gives Qidi and Andrew's noisy fridge comments a frosty reception.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Holly Venning hosts the fourth dinner party from Cumbria and the food marketing manager wants to show her love for her home county through her menu. She prepares Cumbrian scallops and chorizo, followed by a traditional Cumberland sausage - but her Penrith fudge pudding doesn't appeal to everybody.(n)

programme.desc: The shrink's radio show survives a stern focus-group test, but he cannot help wondering why one of the members was less impressed than the others. Meanwhile, Daphne finds the thought of returning to Manchester difficult. Comedy, guest starring Tony Shalhoub (Monk), with Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne tries to avoid returning to England to visit her family, while Frasier is given a recording of his first radio broadcast in Seattle. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, Gillian and husband Steve kick things off with a simple menu and put the emphasis on presentation. Day two sees Holly and Richard lay on a vegetarian meal, which fails to please Gillian. Foodies Jen and Alex host the final night, featuring some rap poetry.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: It's crunch time for two plant nursery entrepreneurs in France as the season comes to an end. A couple who run motocross holidays in Spain deal with their biggest booking, while a cafe owner on the Costa del Sol tries to protect her business battling a flood.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Keen cyclists Andy and Jacqui search for a Spanish holiday home that they and their five children can enjoy, on a budget of £75,000. Presented by Jean Johansson.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits California, where his job is to help the owner of Lido Di Manhattan. Lisa Marie Hemmat, an ambitious business school graduate, has been running the restaurant for the past five years, but has failed to exert any control over the kitchen and the menu. The chef tries to teach her the basics of cooking and venue management, but trouble brews when her staff threaten to walk out on relaunch night.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A look back at some of Noel Fitzpatrick's most memorable cases including 18-month-old chihuahua Darcy, who suffered a crushed pelvis after being hit by a car, rendering her unable to walk. The programme also features three-legged cat Jersey, whose sole back leg was taking so much strain that he needed knee surgery, and deerhound Willow, suffering from a suspected broken neck after a horrific accident.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The 100th episode of the series focuses on the love between patients and their nearest and dearest. Sir John Cockburn, who's 89, arrives at St George's with acute abdominal pain and medics think it could be an abdominal aortic aneurysm and he faces emergency surgery. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Mick and his wife Linda have been involved in a head-on car crash. Mick took the brunt of the impact and has multiple injuries from top to toe that could be life-changing. The couple fell in love after leaving previous relationships, making a happy life together in their mobile home. And 10-year-old Niall is brought to A&E after falling 8ft from a tree on a family picnic. He's broken his arm so badly it looks like a banana, and orthopaedic doctors need to work out how to get it back in place.(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls UK
programme.desc: A distressing call is taken by emergency call handlers when a young woman finds her flat mate murdered in their home in Gateshead. Elsewhere, extreme weather conditions in Kent lead to one of the biggest road traffic accidents in UK history, and in Essex, a five-year-old boy with autism makes an extraordinary call to the emergency services.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Denise Van Outen and her partner Eddie, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Martin Kemp and son Roman, Maureen Lipman and Gyles Brandreth, Johnny Vegas and his PA Bev, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Emilia Fox and Joanna David, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford, Laura Whitmore and Iain Stirling, Rylan Clark-Neal and Linda Clark, Georgia Taylor and Sunetra Sarker and Susanna Reid and Rob Rinder offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows, sparking debate and giving viewers the chance to share those 'we said that' moments.(n)

programme.desc: A woman owes five months' rent, but refuses to leave unless the bailiffs arrive, while a man wanted to sell his house post-breakup, but the tenants are still there five years later. Plus, a man happily living in his garden shed.(n)

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: The documentary examining the lives of individuals on the dole in the UK follows the stories of Roma people who moved to the UK and are now surviving on payments from the welfare state. Ion is from Romania and wants to make £40,000 from benefits to help him build a new house for his family back in his home village, while Katarina arrived from Slovakia seven years ago and has 11 children and 11 grandchildren living in and around an estate in Rotherham, South Yorkshire. Nottingham resident Viorel is 25 and lost both legs in an accident in his native Romania when he was seven, and now wants to pursue a career in the movies.(n)

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: This edition of the programme focuses on large families who depend on the benefits system, including 64-year-old Peter Rolfe, whose children - by 15 different women - range in ages from three to 43, of which he is the sole carer for six. He has been fighting his council in the Isle of Wight for a much bigger house and is also at loggerheads with the benefits office for capping his weekly payment. West Midlands mother-of-eight Marie Buchanan has recently split from her partner and is looking for a larger home, while Ipswich couple the Fisks have been in dispute with their neighbours, who objected to two houses being knocked into one to accommodate them and their 13 children.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.desc: Claire and Simon seek eviction specialist Chris's help to evict a man claiming to be renting their old family home. programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Cases include Wendy, who was left with rental arrears of almost £3,000 after renting a house out to a nightmare tenant. Steve from Birmingham Council's housing enforcement team investigates poor living conditions above a local shop. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

A Staffordshire optician who owes money to a supplier claims that the equipment he was sold was faulty, while enforcement agents in north London are forced to mediate between a tenant who claims she is being evicted for having children and a landlord who says she wanted to leave in the hope of getting a council house.(n)

programme.desc: The team discovers a former fingerprint examiner may have provided false testimony to help the prosecution in several court cases. programme.title: Law & Order

programme.desc: A car chase through Manhattan leaves one man dead and a teenager injured, leading Fontana and Green to discover the victim was being followed after massacring a hunting party. Starring Sam Waterston and Annie Parisse.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A high-school student is found dead in an apartment building, and Briscoe and Green suspect an improper website may have played a part in her murder. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Jesse L Martin and Elisabeth Rohm.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of an investment analyst whose dealings with a bankrupt company left many clients penniless. Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to investigate the murder of a retired US Navy lieutenant commander, and the inquiry forces Ducky to reveal he has been working as part of a secret society dedicated to solving cold cases. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Kyle Friedgen is accused of killing his ex-girlfriend, but while his case is dismissed due to a mishandled warrant, his life and reputation are marred by suspicion that he got away with murder. programme.title: Rookie Blue
programme.desc: The investigation of a missing girl is identified as a possible copycat crime, echoing of one of the squad's most gut-wrenching cases. programme.desc: Fontana and Green are baffled by a series of flu-related deaths until they discover the victims were given bogus vaccines. programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate when a priest is found murdered in the confessional booth. Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.desc: The team investigates a youth hockey team, where bullying of new players has taken a dangerous turn.(n)

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: A bizarre accident causes Geordi LaForge and Ro to become undetectable to everyone but each other. As a result, the crew think they are dead and make arrangements for their funerals, even though they are closer than anyone realises. Sci-fi adventure, starring Patrick Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.desc: The team has to protect a captured serial killer being hunted by an assassin, while a hurricane hits Oahu and Rachel and Charlie evacuate to Danny's house, where they reminisce.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part season finale, Morgana and her massive army close in around Camelot, and when she strikes, the ferocity of her attack forces everyone to run for their lives - even Arthur. But the sorceress will not let him escape that easily - and so begins a chilling hunt for the king. Can Merlin save his master's life yet again? Sword-and-sorcery adventure, starring Colin Morgan, Bradley James and Katie McGrath, with guest appearances by Ben Daniels and Miranda Raison.(n)

programme.title: Frontline Police
programme.desc: Rav Wilding accompanies Essex police on a drugs raid, but the operation turns into a chase when the suspect escapes through the back door. Paramedics rush to Epping Forest to help an unconscious man, an emergency unit struggles to get a woman to the police station, and a routine traffic stop catches a motorist who is three times over the drink-drive limit. Elsewhere, officers search for a man who has been accused of threatening his former girlfriend - and find him in a very unusual place.(n)

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: A burglar is arrested twice within days, a helicopter is used to find a woman heard screaming in a park, and an argument over cigarettes turns nasty.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb helps British couple George and Frances Phipps find a new home on the Costa del Sol for £100,000. Among the properties she suggests are a white house in a Spanish village and a luxury apartment near a marina.(n)

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen seeks a boyfriend for her socially inept elder sister Laura, so invites nervous horticulturalist Mike to a party. The pair discover they share a dislike of being fixed up, so depart together to prevent Helen objecting to them leaving. Bob Larbey's comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams as middle-aged romantics.(n)

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Helen and Phil are keen to know how things went between unlikely lovebirds Laura and Mike, so both end up invited to lunch - where they relate conflicting stories. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams.(n)

programme.title: How to Stay Young
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which Angela Rippon and Dr Chris van Tulleken investigate the latest experiments and research that could help put the brakes on the ageing process. The first programme focuses on the physical aspects, revealing how much is down to how people live their lives and how people's lifestyle choices can help combat the effects of ageing. Chris finds out how public attitudes to the elderly can have a negative impact, spending a day as an 80-year-old with the help of Hollywood-style prosthetics, and Angela visits a study in Germany comparing the benefits of dancing with conventional gym exercises.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the contrasting stories of people responding to the traumas of the Second World War. While Kent student Daphne Baker set her heart on becoming a head teacher as her contribution to the war effort, conman Neville Heath embarked on a fraudulent spree preying on vulnerable women.(n)

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra.(n)

programme.title: All Coppers Are (1971)
programme.desc: A young policeman and a small-time crook are both involved with the same girl. Drama, starring Martin Potter, Julia Foster and David Essex.(n)

programme.title: Blind Corner (1964)
programme.desc: A composer's wife learns her husband plans to cut her out of his will, so conspires with her lover to murder him first. Crime drama, starring William Sylvester and Barbara Shelley.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire.(n)

programme.desc: Prince Charles pays a personal tribute to Her Majesty to mark her 60-year reign. He reflects on various public events and private family moments over the past six decades and shows previously unseen photographs and home movies - some of which were shot by the Queen herself.(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Lady Persie's life has become increasingly complex, as she continues her illicit affair and pursues her right-wing political interests with little regard for the potential danger to those she loves. Meanwhile, Lady Agnes prepares for motherhood by having her picture taken by celebrated photographer Cecil Beaton - a visit that causes excitement for one particular member of the downstairs staff - and Sir Hallam finds himself dragged into the crisis surrounding the abdication of Edward VIII facing the British monarchy. Keeley Hawes, Ed Stoppard, Anne Reid and Claire Foy star in the period drama.(n)

programme.title: Jasper Jones (2017)
programme.desc: When a town outcast suspected of murder shows 14-year-old Charlie Bucktin a dead body, the teenager attempts to find the killer. Mystery thriller, starring Levi Miller, Aaron McGrath and Angourie Rice.(n)

programme.title: Essex Vendetta (2016)
programme.desc: An ordinary man is drawn into the dangerous world of organised crime as he strives to protect his family. His new life in the Essex underworld leads to him being sent on a murderous mission to Malta, where he discovers his partners in crime are not to be trusted. Crime drama, starring Michael McKell, Kris Johnson and Danniella Westbrook.(n)

programme.title: We Still Steal the Old Way (2017)
programme.desc: After their exploits in We Still Kill the Old Way, criminal outfit the Archer Gang encounters an old enemy when a daring heist ends with them behind bars, however that doesn't stop them attempting to break out an old friend. Prison drama, starring Ian Ogilvy, Billy Murray, Julian Glover and Lysette Anthony.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man

programme.desc: Nine men with limited childcare experience, who are either fathers or hope to have children soon, try to improve their skills by working in nurseries, taking on a variety of tasks such as nappy changing and supervising playtime. She finds a way to support herself and her wheelchair-bound grandfather by gathering seaweed along the coast and selling it. Family drama, starring Donald Pleasence, Veronica Quilligan, Peter Vaughan and Arthur English.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White treks along the Isle of Man's stunning coast and visits Sprotbrough in South Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises house-hunters Steve Cox and Varinder Dhami who are looking for a holiday home in Lanzarote, showing them round a countryside villa and a £50,000 apartment.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A new commanding officer pushes the team too hard, leaving the overworked team planning a revolt. Ash and Susan consider their future.(n)

programme.title: A Jubilee Tribute to the Queen by the Prince of Wales
programme.desc: Prince Charles pays a personal tribute to Her Majesty to mark her 60-year reign. He reflects on various public events and private family moments over the past six decades and shows previously unseen photographs and home movies - some of which were shot by the Queen herself.(n)

programme.title: Oasis
programme.desc: Profile of the Gallagher brothers, who formed Oasis in the early 1990s and have gone on to sell more than 70 million albums.(n)

programme.title: The Moodys
programme.desc: As the family takes an Easter trip to a holiday park on the coast, Kev sets up a camp so impressive it earns him an invitation to join the Australian Cruising Drivers Club, while a mistake with the booking leaves Sean and three others facing the weekend in a cramped motorhome. Comedy, starring Patrick Brammall.(n)

programme.title: Content Content
programme.desc: Sitcom based in the offices of Content Content, a digital content agency that nobody, including the employees, can pronounce correctly.(n)

programme.title: Spivs (2004)
programme.desc: A professional con-man spends his time honing the skills of his protege and making sure his gang of conniving criminals stays out of trouble. However, when a simple heist backfires the ragtag group are left with a lorry-load of illegal immigrants on their hands - and an angry smuggler with revenge on his mind soon adds to their woes. Crime drama, starring Ken Stott,, Nick Moran and Dominic Monaghan.(n)

programme.title: Music Within (2007)
programme.desc: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing-impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities. Biographical drama, starring Ron Livingston, Melissa George and Michael Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Mad Dog Morgan (1976)
programme.desc: An Irishman seeking his fortune in the Australian Gold Rush ends up in prison when driven to robbery out of desperation. After 12 years of abuse, he is released and becomes an outlaw, determined to make society pay for his suffering - and becoming a hero to the downtrodden in the process. Fact-based Western, starring Dennis Hopper and Jack Thompson.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the contrasting stories of people responding to the traumas of the Second World War. While Kent student Daphne Baker set her heart on becoming a head teacher as her contribution to the war effort, conman Neville Heath embarked on a fraudulent spree preying on vulnerable women.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams travels to the Wiltshire village home of Jim Stoodley, who at the age of 14 stole a US aircraft and took to the air with the intention of killing Adolf Hitler - earning himself a place in the Guinness Book of Records as the youngest pilot in history.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: A new commanding officer pushes the team too hard, leaving the overworked team planning a revolt. Ash and Susan consider their future.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: First episode of the animated series about a pop group's adventures during a world tour. The girls break down in Transylvania and seek shelter in a mad scientist's castle.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band helps a young fan to drum up publicity for a struggling diner by faking an alien encounter.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Animated series about a young viking. A gang of pirates leads Vic's father into a trap, but the youngster hatches a plan to trick them.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvbar goes in search of a shooting star, but finds it is nothing but a worthless rock. However, Vic works out that it may have very useful magnetic properties.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra challenges the monkeys to a race around the island.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra invites his friends to a sleep-over, but things do not go according to plan.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys start their own printing press, but end up making up their own stories to stay one step ahead of the town crier.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: A diamond crashes into the sea from space, and everyone wants to get their hands on it.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb advises house-hunters Steve Cox and Varinder Dhami who are looking for a holiday home in Lanzarote, showing them round a countryside villa and a £50,000 apartment.(n)

programme.title: Dream the Impossible (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about paraplegic motorcycle racer and paralympic skier Talan Skeels-Piggins, who has founded a charity to train paralysed people to ride adapted motorbikes.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the ration-book fraudsters and coupon fiddlers spawned by the restrictions of the Second World War, and looks at the career of gangster Frankie Fraser, for whom the war provided an opportunity to get rich quick.(n)

programme.title: Earth Cycle
programme.desc: Simon Parker charts the seasonal cycles of the natural world as he journeys through Scandinavia on a bicycle, wild camping, seeing animal migrations and sampling local food.(n)

programme.title: Blind Corner (1964)
programme.desc: A composer's wife learns her husband plans to cut her out of his will, so conspires with her lover to murder him first. Crime drama, starring William Sylvester and Barbara Shelley.(n)

programme.title: All Coppers Are (1971)
programme.desc: A young policeman and a small-time crook are both involved with the same girl. Drama, starring Martin Potter, Julia Foster and David Essex.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing New Cross, Eltham and Catford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the cliffs at Flamborough Head and through Clumber Park, Nottinghamshire.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Amanda Lamb helps Michael Grant search for a holiday home in Fuerteventura on the Canary Islands for £60,000. Properties on offer include a bargain flat, a traditional farmhouse and rural accommodation in the desert.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Susan decides to go back to her husband, while Ash gets some help from the Navy in learning hoe to dispose of new German land mines.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Henry VIII: The Mind of a Tyrant
programme.desc: David Starkey examines how the Tudor king went from being a handsome young prince whose ascension to the throne in 1509 filled the nation with hope to a ruthless monarch.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Kenny prepares to fight in a championship match. Starring Ray Winstone. Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Black Gunn
programme.desc: A member of a black militant group carries out a robbery on a crime syndicate that ends in several deaths. When he is later murdered by the gangsters, his nightclub owner brother swears revenge. Crime thriller, starring Jim Brown and Martin Landau.(n)

programme.title: Family Fortunes

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Noel Edmonds hosts the game show in which contestants try to guess the contents of mystery boxes - which could contain as much as £250,000.(n)

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Noel Edmonds hosts the high-stakes game show, which sees contestants trying to hold their nerve in the hope of walking away with £250,000 - instead of going home with nothing more than a penny.(n)

programme.title: Weakest Link
programme.desc: Anne Robinson hosts the general knowledge quiz in which contestants answer questions as a team, eliminating the poorest players as they go.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Model Lisa Snowdon and actor Will Mellor (Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps) join forces with their relatives to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, in which they can win up to £30,000 for a charity of their choice. Vernon Kay hosts.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)
